title: Alternate formats for the Future Census consultation | Stats NZ
content:
## Alternate formats for the Future Census consultation
The information on this page has been developed to support people who have chosen to take part in the Future Census consultation.
Submissions for the Modernising our approach to the 2028 Census consultation closed at 5pm on 19 June 2024.
For more information or if you have any questions about the consultation, please:
- email 2028census@stats.govt.nz
Alternate formats are available to help you access information about the survey in a way that best meets your needs. Formats available to download on this page include Audio, Braille, Easy Read, Large Print, and New Zealand Sign Language (NZSL).
If you want to speak to Stats NZ over the phone – either to ask questions or submit a response – and you are Deaf, hard of hearing, or speech impaired, the NZ Relay Service can help youmake contact by phone.
NZ Relay has more information about this service.

## What you will find on this page
Audio Braille Easy Read Large Print New Zealand Sign Language (NZSL)
- Audio copy of Modernising our approach to the 2028 Census: Summary of the discussion document for public consultation MP3 audio file, 5.9 MB

### Audio copy of Modernising our approach to the 2028 Census: Summary of the discussion document for public consultation
MP3 audio file, 5.9 MB
- Audio copy of Modernising our approach to the 2028 Census: Discussion document for public consultation MP3 audio file, 20 MB

### Audio copy of Modernising our approach to the 2028 Census: Discussion document for public consultation
MP3 audio file, 20 MB
Audio of the full document, “Modernising our approach to the 2028 Census: Discussion document for public consultation”, and audio of the summary document, “Modernising our approach to the 2028 Census: Summary of discussion document for public consultation”, will also be available on TellMe for the full consultation period.
TellMe is a news service that is over the telephone and smart speakers. You can get in touch by calling 0800 835 563 .
